covered_report_window: Wednesday, July 22, 2026 after U.S. market close plus Thursday, July 23, 2026 before U.S. market open.
market_context_summary: U.S. market was still open at run time. Nasdaq/growth sentiment was fragile ahead of Alphabet and Tesla; oil was up sharply on Iran conflict risk, Treasury/inflation concerns remained live, and AI capex scrutiny was the dominant cross-stock theme.

ranked_companies.timing: Thu Jul 23 BMO / 10:00 a.m. ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Copper strength and a confirmed BMO event create the cleanest upside setup, provided cost and Grasberg/volume commentary do not spoil the move.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Analysts expected roughly $0.59-$0.60 EPS and revenue around $6.7B, with copper-price leverage central.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Higher realized copper, stable costs, constructive volume guidance, and ongoing copper tightness.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Premiums prove tariff-driven rather than demand-driven, costs rise, or mine commentary disappoints.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Market Chameleon showed FCX expected to report BMO and an average implied post-earnings move around 4.4%; commodity beta can overwhelm earnings specifics.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Preview sources pointed to EPS growth despite lower revenue expectations, implying the quality of price/cost leverage matters more than headline revenue.

ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: The warning resets estimates lower, but the stock still needs credible software, consulting, and free-cash-flow repair commentary to avoid another negative reaction.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Reports said IBM warned for $17.2B revenue versus prior $17.9B consensus and adjusted EPS below consensus.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Management frames the warning as temporary, software/AI pipeline holds, and cash-flow guidance is protected.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Deal delays broaden, software growth underwhelms, or investors see AI infrastructure shifts as structural.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Options AI showed an expected move around 6.4%; the large pre-earnings selloff raises relief risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: The warning itself is the dominant revision event; analysts were divided on whether it is execution noise or a deeper issue.

ranked_companies.timing: Wed Jul 22 AMC / 3:30 p.m. CT call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Industrial and data-center recovery supports upside, but the stock likely needs more than an in-line beat after a major run.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Analysts expected about $1.94 EPS and $5.3B revenue; prior Q2 guide was $5.0B-$5.4B revenue and $1.77-$2.05 EPS.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Data-center power demand and industrial recovery remain strong, margins expand, and capex is seen peaking.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Auto softness, inventory caution, or a merely seasonal Q3 guide disappoints a stretched setup.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed an implied move above 10%, and the stock had rallied sharply into the event.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Prior Q1 beat and Q2 guide above consensus raised the bar for another beat-and-raise style outcome.

ranked_companies.timing: Wed Jul 22 AMC / 5:00 p.m. ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: The key issue is whether cRPO and renewal quality prove AI resilience, while margin pressure and software-sector distrust keep downside pressure live.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Analysts expected roughly $0.86 EPS and 22% revenue growth to $3.9B; Q1 cRPO growth was 22.5% but Q2 organic cRPO concerns hurt the stock.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: cRPO clears the lowered bar, AI revenue and renewal rate reassure, and Armis integration looks manageable.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Organic cRPO slows, AI threatens seat-based pricing, or margin pressure persists.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Market Chameleon and TipRanks showed a very large implied move, roughly 12%; severe de-rating creates high squeeze risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 raised subscription revenue guidance, but margin and organic cRPO worries drove negative revisions to sentiment.

ranked_companies.timing: Thu Jul 23 BMO / 8:30 a.m. ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: TMO has a moderate relief-up setup if the raised guide and bioprocessing demand hold, but the market has previously faded clean beats.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Kiplinger listed expected EPS around $5.72; Q1 revenue grew 6% to $11.01B and adjusted EPS grew 6% to $5.44.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Biopharma demand, China, and Clario contribution support the raised full-year guide.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Investors again punish a beat if organic demand or margin quality looks soft.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Options AI showed a 7.2% expected move versus a 3.4% average realized move, implying a demanding event premium.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 raised 2026 revenue and adjusted EPS guidance, giving the setup credible positive revision support.

ranked_companies.timing: Thu Jul 23 BMO / 8:30 a.m. ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Broadband remains the decisive metric, and the stock likely needs continued loss improvement plus credible media-cost control to avoid downside.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Kiplinger listed expected EPS around $0.96; Q1 domestic residential broadband losses were 65,000 while wireless net adds were 435,000.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Broadband losses narrow, wireless stays strong, and NBA/media cost worries ease.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Broadband worsens or media EBITDA pressure offsets revenue growth.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Options evidence was less clean; Market Chameleon historical data showed CMCSA earnings moves can exceed implied ranges.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 beat did not fully remove concern about broadband and media cost pressure.

ranked_companies.timing: Wed Jul 22 AMC / 4:30 p.m. ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Alphabet’s fundamentals look strong, but the stock reaction is balanced between Cloud upside and very high AI capex expectations.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: FactSet-based previews pointed to about $2.88 EPS and $117.1B revenue; buy-side Cloud expectations may be above sell-side consensus.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Cloud growth near or above whisper expectations, Search/YouTube resilience, and capex framed as high-return.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Capex or equity-raise concerns overwhelm a beat, or Cloud growth misses the whisper bar.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: GOOGL expected move ranged roughly 5.6%-7.3% across options sources; capex sensitivity raises reversal risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 Cloud grew 63% and backlog topped $460B, making Cloud the decisive KPI but also raising expectations.

ranked_companies.timing: Wed Jul 22 AMC / 5:30 p.m. ET webcast
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Tesla’s delivery beat supports the quarter, but cash burn and AI-capex credibility make the stock too path-dependent for a clean directional edge.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Analysts expected about $0.53-$0.55 EPS and $26.4B-$27.4B revenue after 480,126 Q2 deliveries and 13.5 GWh storage deployments.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Robotaxi/Optimus updates, delivery upside, and short interest fuel a relief rally.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Auto gross margin, free cash flow, or capex guidance disappoints despite strong deliveries.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Options sources showed roughly 6%-8% implied move; short interest rose, increasing upside reversal risk for bearish calls.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Delivery estimates moved higher, but analysts increasingly focus on AI spending and free cash flow rather than headline vehicle volumes.

ranked_companies.timing: Thu Jul 23 BMO / 8:30 a.m. ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Defense demand is supportive, but Q1 cash-flow weakness and execution risk keep the consensus near Neutral.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Kiplinger listed expected EPS around $7.22; Q1 sales were flat at $18.0B and free cash flow was negative $291M.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Backlog converts, cash-flow guide is reaffirmed, and missile/air-defense demand supports tone.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: F-35/program costs, working capital, or supply-chain issues weigh on guidance.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: OptionSlam showed a weekly implied move around 4.3%; defense geopolitics can buffer downside.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Q1 cash flow disappointed, while full-year demand backdrop and defense backlog kept relief potential alive.

qualified_names.why: Score above 30, reversal risk below 40, low disparity, clear commodity/KPI driver.
Score above 30, reversal risk below 40, low disparity, raised-guide setup.

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Score above 30 but reversal risk 43 after major rally and high implied move.
Strong downside score but reversal risk 49 after large pre-earnings selloff.
Absolute score above 30 but reversal risk 64 from low bar and de-rating.
Score did not clear allocation threshold and reversal risk 58.
High reversal risk 72 and final call Neutral / No Edge.
Reversal risk 45 and score below allocation threshold.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
